commit_message: i18n(vi): translate QR login, password reset & device verification strings
pr_title: i18n(vi): translate device-management, QR login & verification strings
pr_body: ## Summary Fills **146 missing Vietnamese (`vi`) translations** in `apps/web/src/i18n/strings/vi.json` across two commits on this branch, focused on the device-management user flows referenced by issue #24529 ("List of logged in devices uses non-locale-sensitive date formatting"). This PR localises the UI surface around the buggy date display, even though it does not itself change the underlying date-formatting code. Maintainers may merge it in parallel with whatever code-level fix lands for #24529. ### Areas covered **Commit 1 (ea5a369) — 70 keys, settings cluster:** - `settings.encryption.*` — advanced reset flow, key storage, recovery, delete key storage, device-not-verified prompts (59 keys) - `settings.security.*` — analytics description, dehydrated/offline device copy, dialog title, message-search pending rooms (5 keys) - `settings.sessions.*` — best security note, dialog title, error states, manage, sign-in-with-QR unsupported (6 keys) **Commit 2 (586cec9) — 76 keys, device-linking + verification flows:** - `auth.qr_code_login.*` — sign-in with QR code (used to link new devices): 2-digit check code, secure-channel error copy, rate-limit / expired / cancelled errors, unsupported-protocol fallback (35 keys) - `auth.reset_password.*` — post-reset device-logout success/warning copy, rate-limit errors (5 keys) - `encryption.verification.*` — device verification dialogs, manual verification flow, QR reciprocate prompts, emoji comparison, recovery-key fallback (34 keys) - `devtools.crypto.session` + `session_fingerprint` (2 keys) ### Translation quality notes - Formal vous-form (`bạn`) used throughout, consistent with the existing `vi.json`. - All ICU/printf-style placeholders preserved exactly: `%(brand)s`, `%(scanQRCode)s`, `%(deviceId)s`, `%(error)s`, `%(fingerprint)s`, `%(fprint)s`, `%(timeout)s`, `%(pendingRooms)s`, `%(changeRecoveryKeyButton)s`, `...`, `...`. - Technical terms kept untranslated where there is no clean Vietnamese equivalent or where the term is a proper noun: `QR`, `ID`, `IP`, `User ID`, `wifi`, `proxy`, `pusher`, `Markdown`. - Reused existing vocabulary from the file (e.g. `phiên` for session, `xác minh` for verify, `mã hoá` for encryption, `khoá khôi phục` for recovery key, `vân tay` for fingerprint) so the new strings blend with the surrounding UI. - Keys are alphabetically sorted within each object, matching the existing structure. - File preserved as UTF-8 (no BOM), 4-space indent, trailing newline. ### Files changed - `apps/web/src/i18n/strings/vi.json` (+164 / -14 across both commits) Related to #24529. ## Test plan - [x] `python -m json.tool apps/web/src/i18n/strings/vi.json` — passes - [ ] Visual review of QR-login, password-reset, and device-verification UI in Vietnamese locale
notes: Issue #24529 is technically a code-level locale-sensitive date-formatting bug; this PR addresses the translation gap on the same UI surface, not the formatting code itself. Two commits on the branch — ea5a369 (settings cluster, 70 keys) and 586cec9 (device-linking + verification, 76 keys) — total 146 new Vietnamese strings. After merge, vi coverage rises from 2766 → 2912 / 3514 keys (~83%). Remaining gaps are mostly in devtools, labs, location_sharing, voip and poll namespaces — outside the device-list scope and best handled in follow-up PRs. Sort-order normalization of two existing plural blocks (items_and_n_others, n_sessions_selected) in the first commit was a side-effect of json.dump(sort_keys=True); translations were unchanged.
